---
title: "Grimm, Simon, Dr."
description: "Software Engineer"
image: images/simon-grimm.jpg
categories: [staff, all]
about:
  template: jolla
  image-shape: round
  links:
    #see a list of available icons here: https://icons.getbootstrap.com/
    - icon: envelope-at
      text: Email
      href: mailto:sigrimm@ethz.ch 
    - icon: github
      text: Github
      href: https://github.com/sigrimm
    - icon: github
      text: Bitbucket
      href: https://bitbucket.org/sigrimm
    - icon: globe
      text: homepage
      href: "#" 
---

Dr. Simon Grimm obtained his PhD in 2015 from the Institute for Computational Science at the University of Zürich, Switzerland, specializing in the development of high-performance computational methods for astrophysics.

Following his doctoral studies, he served as a Senior Researcher (Oberassistent) at the Center for Space and Habitability at the University of Bern, where he also contributed his expertise as a GPU computing specialist for the university-wide supercomputing team, UBELIX. From 2023 to 2026, Simon continued his research as a Senior Researcher, splitting his time between the Exoplanetary Interiors group at ETH Zürich and the Planet Formation group at the University of Zürich.

In 2026, Simon joined the ETH Zürich Space Hub as a Software Engineer, where he is currently part of the Swiss LISA Data Processing Center. He is the lead author of several widely used simulation codes, including GENGA (a GPU-accelerated N-body integrator for planetary dynamics) and HELIOS-K (a high-performance opacity calculator for exoplanetary atmospheres).
